goal: Add a provider-call executor mount lock that composes activation-lock evidence before any later provider execution activation, without calling providers, executing runtime, mutating routes, writing state, issuing permits, merging, or deploying.
scope_out: Do not call providers, execute runtime, mutate routes, write DB/files/GitHub, issue permits, merge, deploy, change packages, change workflows, or alter retired sandbox write routes.
claims: Bluepilot can preflight provider-call executor mount readiness from Activation-Lock evidence.
The lock requires confirmation, operator execution ref, provider mount ref, provider isolation ref, and activation_lock_ready for provider_call.
Even when executorMountReady is true, providerCallsAllowed remains false.
assumptions: Actual provider execution remains a later explicit env-gated operation.
The provider runtime activation preflight remains contract-only and does not call providers.
dod: GET /probe/provider-call-executor-mount-lock-contract returns the closed mount boundary.
POST /probe/provider-call-executor-mount-lock-preflight validates activation-lock evidence without provider calls.
Focused tests, typecheck, task-lock verification, diff-check, and full builder tests pass.
stop_conditions: Provider call, runtime execution, route mutation, DB write, file write, GitHub write, permit issue, merge, deploy, package change, workflow change, or sandbox write reopening becomes necessary.
prior_task_findings: Runtime Dry-Run Executor Mount Lock is merged and live. Provider calls now need an equivalent executor mount lock before any provider execution activation can be reviewed.
